# LMS Bridge

> LMS Bridge is a free, open-source (AGPL-3.0) AI-guided adaptive-learning tool for
> undergraduate STEM. It turns an LMS's assessment results into just-in-time, pedagogically
> constrained remediation: when a student slips on a concept, it generates a guided AI tutoring
> session that diagnoses the misconception and helps them recover — it never hands over graded
> answers. Institutions self-host it and bring their own AI provider key, so student data stays
> under their control.

## What it is

- Category: educational technology; LTI 1.3 / LTI Advantage tool; adaptive learning / remediation.
- License: GNU AGPL-3.0 (free and open source). No per-student fee, no size limit. Optional paid
  commercial support, managed hosting, and an OEM/commercial license are available.
- Audience: universities and instructors teaching cumulative STEM courses (e.g. computer science,
  data science, statistics), plus EdTech researchers running pilots.
- AI model: provider-agnostic — works with Anthropic, OpenAI, or Azure OpenAI via a
  bring-your-own-key model; a deterministic mock provider runs with no key for demos/tests.

## How it integrates

- Installs into any LTI 1.3 / Advantage LMS — Blackboard, Brightspace, Canvas, and Moodle — from a
  single standards-based integration. Canvas and Moodle support one-click Dynamic Registration.
- Uses LTI Advantage services: SSO launch (OIDC), AGS (grades), NRPS (roster sync), and Deep Linking.
- No LMS shared secrets: LTI 1.3 uses signed keysets (JWKS). The tool publishes its own keys and an
  admin discovers every setup URL from one endpoint (/api/v1/lti/config).
- First launch auto-provisions the user, course, role, and enrollment — no manual roster setup.

## Sage (standalone mode)

- Sage is a standalone mini-LMS with LMS Bridge built in, for instructors who do not run an LMS.
- An instructor signs up, creates a course, authors multiple-choice quizzes, and shares a join code.
- When a student misses a concept on a quiz, a guided remediation session is generated automatically.

## Pedagogy

- Evidence-based strategies: retrieval practice, Socratic scaffolding, and mastery-based progression.
- The tutor diagnoses and guides; it does not provide graded answers and is not a homework-cheating
  tool. Mastery indicators are a private learning aid and do not change instructor-set grades.

## Privacy

- FERPA-aware with role-based access. Runs on the institution's own infrastructure with its own AI
  key, so student data is not sent to a third-party SaaS operated by the project.

## Links

- Homepage: https://www.lmsbridge.app/
- Source code (GitHub, AGPL-3.0): https://github.com/hjmacemail/lmsbridge
- Install guides (per LMS): https://www.lmsbridge.app/#install
- Try the live demo: https://www.lmsbridge.app/#install
